description: HP PC Diagnostics result page ('preuve de test'), a photo of a laptop screen, French UI. Match anchors are STRUCTURAL test-result vocabulary, never a device value (repo is public). Acceptance is config-driven and minimal: a test ran (the signature matched) AND an access-id is PRESENT. The access-id is a PRESENCE check: its mere existence proves the machine reached HP's central system, so a truncated/garbled read still validates; the exact value and the serial are post-processing, not OCR-critical. A BIOS / system-info screen carries none of these anchors -> no match -> human (intruder).
